name: rasa.module.connectors
description: Reference connector pack — ready-made unified connection.json specs + per-API SKILL.md guides (GitHub, Open-Meteo) that install project-owned into .claude/connections/ and pair with rasa.module.http today. Every spec is live-tested and follows the locked SA-031 connection-spec shape exactly: these are the worked examples connector authors copy, and the first entries in the Connections catalog. Once the kernel Connections runtime (SA-031) lands, the same specs become typed conn__ tools with kernel-enforced allowlists.

rasa.note: The first-party connector pack of the Connections track. Specs ship as SEEDS (skip-if-exists) because under contract 1.3.0 + rasa.module.http the consumer's .claude/connections/ copy IS their editable instance (allowlists, account labels, added ops belong to the project after install) — the definition≠instance philosophy at file grain. When the SA-031 kernel loader exists, connector Elements re-home specs to content/connections/ as Element-owned definitions with per-tenant instance records in the kernel; this pack will follow in that major rev. Pairing with rasa.module.http is a SOFT reference (specs are plain data; any SA-031-shaped consumer can read them). GitHub spec declares bearer auth by secret NAME only (github-pat); Open-Meteo is the no-auth worked example. Divergence from SA-031 §1 field names is drift — SA-031 wins.

identity.one_liner: the RasaOS module for connectors and integrations
identity.subject: connectors / integrations
identity.goal: bring connectors / integrations into a project as an installable RasaOS module

element.description: Element-owned files. The connection specs are deliberately NOT here — they seed into project ownership (see seed.files).

files.note: Describes the pack, the seed-vs-content decision, and how to author a new connector from these examples. Fork/author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

The rung-1 Connections catalog index (SA-031 §7 row shape: element/services/type/auth_required/description). Staged here as Element data until registry-public initializes (Phase 8); read by the future GET /v1/connections/catalog. Not installed into consumer projects.
Skill scaffold (opt-in). The generic /sync + /promote below install (directory-mirror); other skills stay opt-in.
Generic /sync — smart-pull upstream Element changes via the kit/<element>/ clone (canon SA-024).
Generic /promote — smart-push local changes back upstream via the kit/<element>/ clone (canon SA-024).

seed.description: Project-owned connection specs copied at install only (skip-if-exists) — never overwritten on Element upgrade. Edit them freely after install; they are yours.

files.note: GitHub REST v3 spec: bearer auth by NAME (github-pat; unauthenticated public reads work rate-limited), 4 curated safe ops (get-rate-limit health probe, get-repo, list-issues, search-repos) + create-issue (safe:false), page-style pagination. Live-tested unauthenticated through rasa.module.http's server.

The 9-section per-API guide: auth modes + HTTP_AUTH_API_GITHUB_COM wiring, rate-limit etiquette (60/hr unauth vs 5000/hr), pagination idiom, X-GitHub-Api-Version pin guidance, write-op cautions, known-errors table.

Open-Meteo spec — the NO-AUTH worked example (auth field omitted entirely). 3 safe ops (geocode health probe, forecast, current-weather) across two allowlisted hosts. Live-tested through rasa.module.http's server.
The 9-section per-API guide: geocode→forecast recipe, variable-name gotchas (temperature_2m etc.), the two-host note, no-key/commercial-use terms.

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). When co-installed under a parent that also stamps a lockfile, the parent's is authoritative (single-writer-per-path, Lock L-004). Substitutes the standard {{ELEMENT_*}} / {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init.

The DEPLOYMENT identity layer (SA-025) — project-owned; what this project is, for whom, on what platform. bin/init generates the element layer (.claude/rasa-identity.md) from rasa.identity every install.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init by the declarative installer. None needed — the seeds carry the directory structure.
